title: Cozy Chinese Cabbage Shui Zhu Yu
description: A pungent and tender Chinese dish featuring cabbage, this recipe is an ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly version of the classic Shui Zhu Yu, with a rich deglaze-fond adding depth to the cozy meal. It is made with a variety of ingredients, including cabbage, fish, and spices.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add sliced ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute, until lightly browned.
Add chopped Chinese cabbage and cook until it starts to wilt, about 2 minutes.
Add fish fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until cooked through.
Remove the fish from the skillet and set aside.
Add soy sauce, rice vinegar, salt, and black pepper to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the skillet.
Return the fish to the skillet and spoon some of the sauce over the top of each fillet.
Garnish with chopped scallions and serve immediately.
